clinical_coherence.notes: 27F left-handed Hispanic with childhood absence epilepsy history and new-onset temporal lobe seizures. Classic right temporal lobe semiology with olfactory aura, behavioral arrest, and left hand automatisms. PCOS and oral contraceptive use add realistic comorbidity. The history of childhood absence epilepsy transitioning to temporal lobe epilepsy is an interesting but less common progression. Exam is appropriately normal inter-ictally.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG shows right anterior temporal sharp waves. MRI shows right hippocampal sclerosis. Video-EEG captures seizures with right temporal onset. PET shows right temporal hypometabolism. Neuropsych shows material-specific visual memory deficit (right temporal). All concordant. Drug interaction check for lamotrigine appropriately notes hormonal contraceptive interaction.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G40.109 correct. Differential includes PNES, migraine aura, and panic disorder with appropriate distinguishing features. Critical actions correctly note the lamotrigine-OCP interaction (lamotrigine levels decrease with estrogen-containing OCP). Contraindicated actions include not prescribing enzyme-inducing AEDs without addressing OCP interaction.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic right TLE presentation with concordant findings. Left-handedness adds slight interest regarding language lateralization for surgical planning. Straightforward difficulty is appropriate.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 27F Hispanic with childhood absence epilepsy history and PCOS is realistic. The epilepsy evolution from childhood absence to adult TLE, while uncommon, is documented.

educational_value.notes: Excellent teaching case for right temporal lobe epilepsy, lamotrigine-OCP interaction, language lateralization considerations in left-handed patients, and olfactory aura localization. The drug interaction is particularly high-yield for clinical practice.

issues_found: Minor: childhood absence epilepsy transitioning to TLE is uncommon and might benefit from a brief note in the case
strengths: Lamotrigine-OCP drug interaction is clinically important and well-integrated
Olfactory aura is a distinct temporal lobe localization feature
